	Test  Plan  Name: CEE_Integ_RCopy_B648
	Test  Ring  Name: mockring1

	Execution  End: 2024-03-01 09:15:00.000000 UTC
	Triager: test.reporter1@hpe.com
	Controller  Types: Merlin2S
	Number  Of  Nodes: 2

	NFS Path: /net/cxo-odin-nfs/exports/odin01/fake1abc123/2024-03-01_08-00-00_111111
	Odin  Link: https://cxo-odin.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net/bi/result/fake001
	Signature: Remote copy FC link failed to synchronize after failover

Bug Summary:
    RCopyFCBasic: TermError: Remote copy FC link failed to synchronize after failover

Term Error:
    *** TermError: Remote copy FC link failed to synchronize after failover. LUN WWN:60002AC000000000000011110007FAAA

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin2S/C650 (primera)
    Host: mockring1.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net
    Host type: rhel8.2/ProLiant DL380 Gen9
    Found: SN1600Q, fw:9.06.02 (d0d5), drv:10.02.01.00.a14-k1

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: Looked at the FC port logs. There are repeated CRC errors on port 1:2:1 around the failure time. This looks like a fabric-level issue causing the RC sync to time out. Assigning to the FC/driver team for further investigation.

Comments.creator: fc.driver.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Reproduced internally. The root cause is an incorrect timeout value set during failover in the FC path recovery logic. Fix: update rcopy_retrans_timeout KVAR and ensure port re-registration completes before sync resumes. Patch in progress at reviewboard r/88100.

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: Verified fix on internal build. Running 3 more regression cycles to confirm stability across different fabric configurations.

Comments.creator: test.reporter2@hpe.com
Comments.text:
Test  Name: testsrc/VolumeManager/PDTest/PDScrubCycle/PDScrubCycle.py

	Odin  Link: https://cxo-odin.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net/bi/result/fake002
	Signature: PD scrubbing operation aborted unexpectedly mid-cycle causing volume inconsistency

Bug Summary:
    PDScrubCycle: TermError: PD scrub cycle aborted on PD 0:1:3

Term Error:
    *** TermError: PD scrub cycle aborted unexpectedly on PD 0:1:3

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin1S/C630 (primera)
    Host: mockring2.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net
    Found: SN1600Q, fw:9.06.02 (d0d5), drv:10.02.01.00.a14

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: I believe this falls under enclosure management. The PD scrub abort correlates with an enclosure temperature spike logged just before the failure. Routing to the enclosure management team to review thermal event handling.

Comments.creator: enclosure.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Confirmed thermal event on cage 1 at the same timestamp. The firmware does not gracefully pause scrubbing during temperature warnings. Fix: add a scrub-pause handler on thermal threshold event. ETA for patch: next sprint.

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: I have analyzed the bug. I feel like we need more runs - just regular runs are okay to confirm whether the scrub abort can happen independently of thermal events.

Comments.creator: test.reporter3@hpe.com

	Odin  Link: https://cxo-odin.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net/bi/result/fake003
	Signature: System Manager restart stalled during node state synchronization

Bug Summary:
    SMRestart: TermError: SM node state sync timed out after 300 seconds

Term Error:
    *** TermError: SM node state sync timed out. Node 1 not responsive.

Host Info:
    Array type: Tornado/8440 (3parH2)
    Host: mockring3.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: Root cause identified: a race condition in the SM state machine where node 1 does not re-register after a clean restart if node 0 has already advanced past the INIT state. Fix exists in reviewboard r/88001. Patch tested on 3 rings without recurrence.

Comments.creator: qa.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Verified fix on 3.3.1.650 internal build. All 5 regression runs passed. Recommending promotion.

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: Known limitation with the current VVol RC implementation. When vCenter loses connectivity and reconnects, the VASA provider does not re-query the sync group state. Enhancement requested to add a reconnect-triggered re-sync event. Logging for tracking only at this stage.

Comments.creator: test.reporter5@hpe.com

Test  Name: testsrc/Serviceability/EventInfra/AlertThreshold/AlertThreshold.py

	Controller  Types: Merlin1S
	Number  Of  Nodes: 2
	Failure  Type: software
	Build  Version: 3.3.1.648
	Signature: Alert not triggered when disk utilization exceeded configured threshold of 90%

Bug Summary:
    AlertThreshold: Alert not fired for disk utilization > 90% threshold

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin1S/C630 (primera)
    Host: mockring5.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: Root cause: off-by-one error in the threshold comparison in alertmgr.c. The alert was coded as >=91 due to integer rounding instead of >=90. One-line fix. Patch submitted to reviewboard r/77005.

Comments.creator: qa.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Fix verified. Alert now fires correctly at >=90%. Closing bug.

Comments.creator: test.reporter6@hpe.com

Test  Name: testsrc/RemoteCopy/IPTest/RCopyIPFailover/RCopyIPFailover.py
	Test  Plan  Name: CEE_Integ_RCopy_B648_IP
	Test  Ring  Name: mockring6

	Odin  Link: https://cxo-odin.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net/bi/result/fake006
	Signature: IP Remote Copy group stuck in FAILOVER_PENDING state after network blip

Bug Summary:
    RCopyIPFailover: RCOPY group stuck in FAILOVER_PENDING indefinitely

Term Error:
    *** TermError: RCopy group mockRCG1 stuck in FAILOVER_PENDING for > 600 seconds

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin2S/C650 (primera)
    Host: mockring6.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: Reproduced twice. The FAILOVER_PENDING state is not cleaned up when the failover does not complete within the timeout. The state machine needs a forced-transition to FAILOVER_FAILED after timeout. Reviewing rcopy state machine code.

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: I have analyzed the bug. I feel like we need more runs - just regular runs are okay to confirm frequency. Workaround: run 'setrcopygroup resync <groupname>' after detecting the PENDING state to manually clear it.

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: Display-only issue in the CLI formatting layer. Actual allocated size is correct in backend. The showvv command rounds down incorrectly for sizes not multiples of 256 MiB. Fix submitted via RTI.

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: RTI accepted. Fix included in 3.3.1.651. Closing.

Test  Name: testsrc/SystemManager/TaskManager/TaskQueueOverflow/TaskQueueOverflow.py
	Test  Plan  Name: CEE_Integ_SM_Task_B648

	Number  Of  Nodes: 4

	NFS Path: /net/cxo-odin-nfs/exports/odin08/fake8hij890/2024-03-20_07-00-00_888888
	Odin  Link: https://cxo-odin.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net/bi/result/fake008
	Signature: Task Manager queue overflow causes SM to stop accepting new tasks

Bug Summary:
    TaskQueueOverflow: SM stopped accepting new tasks after queue depth exceeded 512

Term Error:
    *** TermError: SM task queue full. New tasks rejected.

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin2S/C650 (primera)
    Host: mockring8.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: I think this comes under the system manager task scheduler team. The queue limit of 512 is a fixed compile-time constant set for older hardware. On 4-node configurations under heavy load this is easily exceeded. Routing to SM scheduler owners.

Comments.creator: sm.scheduler.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Confirmed. Fix: make task queue depth configurable via KVAR sm_task_queue_max and increase default to 2048 for 4+ node systems. Patch under review at r/89002.

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: Need more runs to determine if default 2048 is sufficient. Running regular regression on 4-node rings.

Comments.creator: test.reporter9@hpe.com

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin1S/C630 (primera)

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: Root cause: SAS LSI firmware does not flush its write buffer during a bus reset sequence causing partial writes to appear inconsistent. Fix: update to LSI FW version 24.00.00.00. Patch propagated to 3.2.2 MU6 branch.

Comments.creator: qa.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Verified with LSI FW 24.00.00.00. No data inconsistency observed across 10 reset cycles. Marking VERIFIED.

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: Investigating slow role switch. Delay occurs during volume resync phase when the RCopy group contains more than 50 VVs. The resync is serialized rather than parallelized - known architectural limitation being revisited.

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: I have analyzed the bug. I feel like we need more runs just to determine if slowness is consistent or depends on VV count and data delta. Running regular regression with varying group sizes.

Whiteboard: external only - should not appear in HPE engineer filter

Test  Name: testsrc/RemoteCopy/FCTest/RCopyFCMultiPath/RCopyFCMultiPath.py
	Test  Plan  Name: CEE_Integ_RCopy_FC_465
	Test  Ring  Name: mockring13
	Execution  Start: 2024-04-15 07:00:00.000000 UTC
	Execution  End: 2024-04-15 08:30:00.000000 UTC
	Triager: test.reporter13@hpe.com
	Controller  Types: Tornado
	Number  Of  Nodes: 4
	Failure  Type: software
	Build  Version: 4.6.5
	NFS Path: /net/cxo-odin-nfs/exports/odin10/fake13mno131/2024-04-15_07-00-00_131313
	Odin  Link: https://cxo-odin.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net/bi/result/fake013
	Signature: RC FC multipath failover loses all active paths when two switches fail simultaneously

Bug Summary:
    RCopyFCMultiPath: RC group lost all paths during dual-switch failure

Term Error:
    *** TermError: All paths to RC target lost. RC group mockRCGFC2 degraded.

Host Info:
    Array type: Tornado/8440 (3parH2)
    Host: mockring13.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: Corner case - simultaneous failure of both FC switches removes all paths. The RC path selection algorithm does not handle zero-path state gracefully and marks the group as immediately failed rather than entering a retry state.

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: Proposed fix: add a zero-path recovery state in the RC FC path manager that retries path discovery every 30 seconds for up to 10 minutes before declaring the group failed. This avoids immediate degradation under transient dual-switch failure.

Comments.creator: test.reporter14@hpe.com

Host Info:

    Host: mockring14.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: I think this comes under the CLI parser team. The -d flag triggers a detailed VV info fetch that is not paginated. Above 5000 VVs the response buffer overflows and CLI hangs. Routing to CLI parser owners.

Comments.creator: cli.parser.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Confirmed. Fix adds automatic pagination when VV count exceeds 1000. Patch in review at r/90123. Workaround: use 'showvv -d | head -n 200' to limit output.

Comments.creator: test.reporter15@hpe.com

Test  Name: testsrc/VolumeManager/VLUN/VLUNPresentation/VLUNPresentation.py
	Test  Plan  Name: CEE_Integ_VLUN_332

	Triager: test.reporter15@hpe.com
	Controller  Types: Merlin2S
	Number  Of  Nodes: 4
	Failure  Type: software
	Build  Version: 3.3.2.100
	Signature: VLUN mapping inconsistency after host initiator group modification

Bug Summary:
    VLUNPresentation: VLUN map inconsistent after modifying host initiator group membership

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin2S/C650 (primera)

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: Root cause: when a WWN is removed from a host initiator group, the VLUN export table is not immediately updated. The stale entry persists until the next SM refresh (default 60s). Fix: trigger an immediate VLUN table refresh on initiator group modification. Patch at r/78234.

Comments.creator: qa.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Fix verified. VLUN table updates within 1 second of initiator group modification. Marking VERIFIED.

Comments.text: I have analyzed the bug. I feel like we need more runs to determine if this is port-specific or affects all FC ports. Possible fix: update LIP handler to always reset port state regardless of active IO.

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: Root cause: VASA provider returns HTTP 200 before completing VVol registration in vCenter. The async registration call is not awaited. Fix: make registration synchronous before sending response. RTI submitted.

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com

	Controller  Types: Merlin2S
	Number  Of  Nodes: 4
	Failure  Type: software
	Build  Version: 4.5.0.200
	NFS Path: /net/cxo-odin-nfs/exports/odin17/fake19vwx191/2024-05-01_08-30-00_191919
	Odin  Link: https://cxo-odin.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net/bi/result/fake019
	Signature: Web UI performance graphs fail to load on 4-node system dashboard

Bug Summary:
    DashboardLoadTest: Performance graph widget fails to render on 4-node system

Term Error:
    JavaScript TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'nodePerf') in dashboard.js:1204

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin2S/C650 (primera)
    Host: mockring19.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: Root cause: dashboard.js assumes max 2 nodes when building the nodePerf structure. On 4-node systems, nodes 3 and 4 return undefined. Fix: change fixed 2-element array to a dynamic map keyed by node ID. Fix exists in PR #4421.

Comments.creator: qa.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Fix verified on 2-node, 4-node, and 8-node configurations. Dashboard renders correctly in all cases.

	Signature: iSCSI RC Transport fails to reconnect after target portal reboot

Bug Summary:
    RCTiSCSIReconnect: iSCSI RC session does not re-establish after target portal restart

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin1S/C630 (primera)
    Host: mockring20.cxo.storage.hpecorp.net

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: I think this comes under RC transport/iSCSI session management. The iSCSI initiator does not retry login phase after a target portal restart if the TCP connection is reset cleanly (FIN not RST). The retry logic only triggers on RST. Routing to iSCSI session team.

Comments.creator: iscsi.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Confirmed. Fix: also start the reconnect timer on clean TCP FIN from target portal. Patch under review.

Whiteboard: external only - should not appear in HPE engineer filter

	Signature: Snapshot expiry does not reclaim space when snap count exceeds retention limit

Bug Summary:
    SnapExpiry: Space not reclaimed when snapshots exceed retention policy count limit

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin2S/C650 (primera)

Comments.creator: external.engineer@example.com
Comments.text: The snap expiry daemon marks snapshots for deletion but the space reclamation job (snapclean) is not triggered automatically afterward. Fix: invoke snapclean inline after expiry marks snaps for deletion instead of waiting for the next scheduled run.

	Signature: RC IP group reports data inconsistency at > 5% packet loss

Bug Summary:
    RCopyIPConsistency: RC IP group data inconsistency under 5% packet loss

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin2S/C650 (primera)

Comments.creator: akshay.shankar@hpe.com
Comments.text: Root cause: RC IP transport does not retransmit missing packets when loss rate exceeds 3%. Fix: increase retransmission window and add packet-loss detection KVAR rcopy_ip_retx_threshold. Patch propagated to 3.2.2 MU6 branch.

Comments.creator: qa.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Verified. With 10% simulated packet loss RC group stays consistent across 5 test runs. Marking VERIFIED.

	Signature: Enhancement: PD spare promotion should prefer drives with matching RPM and capacity

Bug Summary:
    PDSparePromotion: Spare PD selected by capacity only, ignoring RPM class match

Host Info:
    Array type: Merlin1S/C630 (primera)

Comments.creator: vasanthakumar2@hpe.com
Comments.text: Enhancement: spare selection algorithm should score by (1) capacity match, (2) RPM match, (3) drive type (SSD > NL-SAS > SAS). Currently only capacity is considered. Patch adds weighted scoring. Fixed on 3.4.0 branch.

Comments.creator: qa.team@hpe.com
Comments.text: Enhancement verified. Mixed RPM pool now correctly promotes the RPM-matching spare first. Closing.
